$policy: See docs/design/execution/chunk-0a/anchors.md §3.3 and docs/design/execution/chunk-0a/brief.md §3.3 (R12 / plan §15 rule 5). Every file under tests/fixtures/goldens/** (excluding this registry itself and fixture-refs.ts modules) must have exactly one entry below. designation is one of: "frozen-migration-input" (the asset is a preservation oracle — a chunk that touches its surface must reproduce it byte-for-byte/outcome-for-outcome; never re-recorded outside its owning chunk) or "re-baseline" (the asset deliberately baselines behavior a later chunk is going to change on purpose; reBaselineChunk names the manifest chunk id — see docs/design/akm-0.9.0-chunk-manifest.json — where it is reviewed and re-captured). reBaselineChunk is required iff designation is "re-baseline" and must be absent otherwise. consumers[] lists the suite(s) that load the fixture via tests/_helpers/golden.ts's expectGolden/loadGolden. Policed by tests/goldens-designations.test.ts. | Amendment (2026-07-16 gate hardening): (1) every frozen-migration-input entry carries a sha256 of its asset bytes, verified by scripts/lint-goldens-presence.ts on every lint run — AKM_UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cannot silently mutate a frozen oracle; a legitimate re-baseline updates designation+reBaselineChunk+sha256-removal in the same reviewed change. (2) Surface-owner rule: a chunk that the plan orders to change an asset's pinned surface becomes that asset's re-baseline owner — re-designate the entry (designation, reBaselineChunk, notes) BEFORE the surface change lands, so the re-capture is a reviewed diff, not a frozen-asset violation.

entries.notes: WI-03, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R3 (engines 1+2 of 3: proposal accept/revert + reject). Preservation oracle through Chunk 6's collapse into one FileChange transaction. Pins accept (new-asset, overwrite-with-backup, idempotent re-accept short-circuit, target-mutated-during-displace abort), revert (success restore, refuse-clobber), reject (success, non-pending UsageError, concurrent-edit succeeds -- reject has NO before-hash), as file-tree/DB-status/exactly-once-event outcomes only -- no journal bytes or paths (brief §3.2 rule 4). Contains fixture-local names only (tests/fixtures/goldens/journal/fixture-refs.ts) — no production refs. Caveat: the `fileTree` object's keys are relative asset paths (e.g. `lessons/<fixture-local-name>.md`) that embed the same fixture-local name a ref would carry; if Chunk 5's §15.2 ref-grammar codemod changes how names map to on-disk paths, these path keys (not the outcome booleans/counts) would need re-baselining alongside that migration even though this asset is otherwise designated frozen. Also records a captured DEVIATION from the brief's testsFirst description for the target-mutated-during-displace scenario (see the suite's file-header comment): the real surfaced abort error is 'Cannot roll back proposal transaction: <path> diverged.', not 'Proposal target changed while its backup was being acquired.', and the target is left holding the mutator's content rather than being byte-identically restored. | Split 2026-07-16 (surface-owner rule, pre-WI-6.4): the createProposal dedup/cooldown/force skip-record shapes moved to tests/fixtures/goldens/journal/proposal-skip-shapes.json (re-baseline @6) because the §23.6 fingerprint scheme legitimately changes them; the accept/revert/reject outcome scenarios in THIS asset stay frozen through the engine swap. sha256 re-pinned over the trimmed bytes in the same reviewed change.

Split 2026-07-16 out of the frozen journal/proposal-txn.json under the registry's surface-owner rule, ahead of Chunk 6's WI-6.4; it then documented the OLD F-2 guard's skip-record shapes (duplicate_pending / content_hash_match / cooldown / force bypass). RE-CAPTURED at WI-6.4 (Chunk 6, its owning chunk — reviewed diff + ledger entry) with the §23.6 input-fingerprint guard's observable contract: fingerprint_match on identical inputs (vs a pending AND vs a rejected proposal — the row survives the lifecycle), newInputsAfterTargetChange (target before-hash rotation queues a second proposal alongside — duplicate_pending is retired), the engine/model-id fingerprint term, rejection_backoff (windows retained verbatim: reflect 14 d / distill 30 d / default 7 d), and force bypass (which still records the fingerprint). Designation returned to frozen-migration-input with a fresh sha256 at WI-6.5 per the same-chunk promise. Contains fixture-local names only (tests/fixtures/goldens/journal/fixture-refs.ts) — no production refs.

WI-03, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R3 (crash-recovery half of engines 1+2 of 3). Integration scope (brief §3.4) — parameterizes the existing, unmodified tests/integration/_helpers/proposal-crash-runner.ts subprocess harness. Pins SIGKILL recovery outcomes for accept (all 5 pre-commit phases: prepared rolls back, every later phase rolls forward), revert (exactly-once per phase), reject (exactly-one rejected event per phase incl. the prepared/state-persisted/event-finalized set), and the reject-recovers-a-pending-accept ordering (akmProposalReject recovers an interrupted accept transaction BEFORE evaluating its own pending-status guard). journalPhasesObserved is informational only (brief §3.2 rule 4) — the single phase name the runner was told to hold at, never journal bytes/paths. Same fileTree-key caveat as tests/fixtures/goldens/journal/proposal-txn.json does not apply here (this fixture serializes only booleans/status strings/event counts, no fileTree). | RE-CAPTURED at WI-6.5 (Chunk 6, its owning chunk — reviewed diff + ledger entry) after the proposal journals moved onto the unified FileChange transaction engine (src/core/fs-txn.ts): phase vocabulary and every recovery outcome came through the swap unchanged — the re-capture diff touched only capturedAtHead, the notes strings, and serializer whitespace. Designation returned to frozen-migration-input with a fresh sha256 in the same reviewed change.

WI-04, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R3 (engine 3 of 3: mv move engine). Preservation oracle through Chunk 6's collapse into one FileChange transaction. Pins the move-with-citers scenario (body-ref + frontmatter-ref + task-yaml citers + .derived.md twin: fileTree, rewrote counts, readOnlyCiters, exactly-one mv event), the divergent-citer abort at both the STAGE (mv-cli.ts:576) and REPLACE (:632) windows, the divergent-committed-target recovery refusal (validateCommittedMove :453-474, roll-forward-only per :1394-1395), and the transient index re-key failure retaining the journal until a later mutation completes it forward -- all as file-tree/citer-rewrite/exactly-once-event outcomes only, no journal bytes or paths (brief §3.2 rule 4). Contains fixture-local names only (tests/fixtures/goldens/journal/fixture-refs.ts) — no production refs. | RE-BASELINED at Chunk 5's §15.2 ref-grammar flip (F5 — this asset's caveat, recorded when it was frozen, named exactly this migration as its re-baseline trigger; surface-owner rule makes Chunk 5 the owner). Ruling-A pure-spelling re-baseline: the diff is PROVABLY spelling-only. (1) `moveWithCiters.from`/`to` flip from legacy `memory:…` to the new-grammar conceptId `memories/…` (mv-cli.ts now echoes displayRef in the JSON envelope + the durable mv-event ref). (2) The three citer fileTree hashes (`knowledge/mv-move-body-citer.md`, `knowledge/mv-move-frontmatter-citer.md`, `tasks/mv-move-nightly.yml`) change because the seeded citer bodies now carry new-grammar refs, which the mv citer-rewrite rewrites to new-grammar targets — verified behaviorally by the suite's own citer-content assertions ('See <toRef> for details.' / '- <toRef>' / exact task-yaml prompt), with toRef = memories/projectA/mv-move-renamed. (3) The moved source + `.derived` twin hashes (no embedded ref) are BYTE-IDENTICAL across the re-baseline, and every outcome boolean/count (rewrote counts 1×3, readOnlyCiters, exactly-one mv event) is unchanged — confirming nothing but the ref spelling moved. Also records a captured DEVIATION from the brief's testsFirst description (see the suite's file-header comment): `_setMvMutationHookForTests` never fires between the stage and replace windows (only post-filesystem-commit), so spyOn interception is used instead; and the REPLACE window's real surfaced error is a wrapped 'Move failed (refusing to replace divergent citer <path>) and rollback failed (cannot restore <path>: file diverged after exclusive ownership).' string, not a bare prefix.

WI-04, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R3 (crash-recovery half of engine 3 of 3). Integration scope (brief §3.4) — parameterizes the existing, unmodified tests/integration/_helpers/mv-crash-runner.ts subprocess harness. Pins SIGKILL recovery outcomes: 'applying' rolls back in full (byte-identical tree, before any citer/source rename); filesystem-committed/index-finalized/state-finalized/event-finalized each roll forward via finalizeMoveTransaction to the same end state with exactly-one mv event and re-keyed index + state rows; and the FOUR independent recovery entry points (mv run's own pre-flight recovery mv-cli.ts:486, proposal promotion repository.ts:1791, the full indexer indexer.ts:560, the targeted write-path indexer index-written-assets.ts:74) each independently finish the SAME kind of interrupted mv journal via recoverTxnsForRoot with a kind === 'mv' filter. journalPhasesObserved is informational only (brief §3.2 rule 4). No ref-grammar caveat: this fixture serializes only booleans/counts/phase-name strings, no fileTree or ref literals. | RE-CAPTURED at WI-6.5 (Chunk 6, its owning chunk — reviewed diff + ledger entry) after the mv journal moved onto the unified FileChange transaction engine (src/core/fs-txn.ts): the old dual journal-home story (in-stash .akm/mv-transactions vs getDataDir() proposal journals) is GONE — every kind's journal now lives in the engine's per-root namespace (getDataDir()/txn/<hash(canonical stash root)>), and the four entry points pin that mv recovery still fires from all four call sites after the collapse. Phase vocabulary and every recovery outcome came through the swap unchanged — the re-capture diff touched only capturedAtHead, the notes strings, and serializer whitespace. Designation returned to frozen-migration-input with a fresh sha256 in the same reviewed change.

WI-05, plan §15.7 / R5. Chunk 7 DoD 5 oracle for the four consolidate op-handlers (handleMergeOp/handleDeleteOp/handlePromoteOp/handleContradictOp, consolidate.ts:2117/2416/2477/2693) extracted out of akmConsolidateInner's op-execution loop — must stay green through Chunk 7's decomposition of that loop. Pins merge (1+1 primary byte-for-byte after <TS> norm + secondary archived-with-superseded_by-then-deleted; 1+2 secondaries' deliberate one-generateMergedContent-call/all-archived asymmetry, captured via the REAL generateMergedContent with _setChatCompletionForTests stubbed since the asymmetry is a property of that function's secondaries[0]-only read; the refusal matrix for hot/unparseable participants and missing/truncated description and the anti-collapse generation guard), delete (normal/hot-refused/already-gone), promote (happy path + the six named gates in consolidate.ts:2477-2690 order), and contradict (>=0.92 byte-for-byte timestamp-free, idempotent re-run, archived state preserved, <0.92 skip, missing confidence defaults to 1.0). Contains fixture-local names only (tests/fixtures/goldens/consolidate/fixture-refs.ts) — no production refs. Archive filenames embed a timestampForFilename() token and are recorded as array VALUES (never object keys) so <TS> normalization applies. DEVIATION from the brief's step 2 prose (see the suite's file-header comment): most scenarios inject a generateMergedContentFn directly into ConsolidateOpContext and drive the handlers directly (mirroring tests/commands/consolidate/consolidate-op-handlers.test.ts) rather than stubbing chunk-plan selection via akmConsolidate + _setChatCompletionForTests — WI-05 scopes to op OUTCOMES, not op SELECTION (planConsolidation's concern, covered by consolidate-chunks.test.ts and WI-06). The 'hot memories via remember hot-capture path' step is satisfied by writing captureMode:hot/beliefState:asserted frontmatter directly (matching remember-cli.ts:188,288's own output shape) rather than invoking the rememberCommand CLI entry point, consistent with how all 13 existing consolidate suites mint hot fixtures. | RE-PINNED at WI-8.5b (Chunk 8, surface-owner rule — stays frozen-migration-input with a fresh sha256, reviewed diff, mirroring the WI-6.5 re-capture precedent): the consolidate provenance-xref WRITERS flip to the D-R5 new grammar — injectGenerationFrontmatter's merged-primary `xrefs` and handlePromoteOp's promoted-body `xrefs` both emit `displayRef`'s short conceptId now. The diff is PROVABLY spelling-only: the merge scenario's `primaryRawBytes` xrefs (memory:cop-merge11-primary/secondary, memory:merge11-existing) and the promote scenario's `bodyXrefs` (memory:cop-promote-happy, memory:promote-happy-existing) flip `memory:<n>` -> `memories/<n>`; every outcome boolean/count and the untouched `superseded_by`/`contradictedBy` frontmatter fields are byte-identical. Two lines change. | RE-PINNED at WI-8.5c (Chunk 8, surface-owner rule — stays frozen-migration-input with a fresh sha256, reviewed diff): the consolidate op refs + derived lesson/knowledge refs flip to the D-R5 conceptId grammar (the parseStoredRef retirement makes op parsing new-grammar-only). The diff is PROVABLY spelling-only — every op ref, xref, and derived ref flips `<type>:<name>` -> `<stash-subdir>/<name>`; all outcome booleans/counts and untouched frontmatter are byte-identical (longer conceptIds re-wrap some inline JSON arrays, no content change).

WI-05, plan §15.7 / R5. Pure-function preservation oracle for mergePlans (consolidate/merge.ts:35, precedence rules :80-110): hallucinated-ref drop (primary and secondary), merge-wins-over-delete (both chunk orders), promote-queued-before-merge (ops ARRAY ORDER, since applyConsolidationPlan processes ops in array order), and contradict pair-dedup by ref|contradictedByRef (keeping the first occurrence). Byte-for-byte JSON in/out — mergePlans has no fs/LLM/config/timestamp/id dependency, so no <TS>/<ID> placeholders apply; this is the one WI-05 asset with zero normalization surface. Contains fixture-local names only. Extends (does not duplicate) tests/commands/consolidate/consolidate-promote-dedup.test.ts's mergePlans promote-by-ref dedup coverage. | RE-PINNED at WI-8.5c (Chunk 8, surface-owner rule — stays frozen-migration-input with a fresh sha256, reviewed diff): the consolidate op refs + derived lesson/knowledge refs flip to the D-R5 conceptId grammar (the parseStoredRef retirement makes op parsing new-grammar-only). The diff is PROVABLY spelling-only — every op ref, xref, and derived ref flips `<type>:<name>` -> `<stash-subdir>/<name>`; all outcome booleans/counts and untouched frontmatter are byte-identical (longer conceptIds re-wrap some inline JSON arrays, no content change).

WI-06, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R5. Preservation oracle for the consolidate checklist journal's full-run write/complete/cleanup lifecycle and the all-hot chunk early-exit (zero LLM calls, every memory bucketed judgedNoAction). RE-CAPTURED at WI-6.3e (Chunk 6, its owning chunk — reviewed diff + ledger entry; designation finalized at WI-6.5): the checklist journal now rides the unified FileChange transaction engine (src/core/fs-txn.ts, kind `consolidate`, root = stash, backups under the transaction dir), observed via spyOn(fs.writeFileSync/copyFileSync) interception of the engine's durable journal.json.tmp writes around a REAL akmConsolidate run — same src/-change-free technique as WI-04's goldens-mv-txn.test.ts. Journal write shape/count is informational data only (brief §3.2 rule 4) — never asserted against raw journal bytes or directory layout, only observable outcomes (write count — 3 under the engine's begin/mark/commit, ledgered delta from the legacy 2 — parsed completed/operations arrays, end-state namespace cleanliness). Caveat (same class as the WI-03/04 fileTree-key caveats): fullRunLifecycle.firstJournalWrite/lastJournalWrite.operations[].ref embeds a fixture-local ref (tests/fixtures/goldens/consolidate/fixture-refs.ts) that would need re-keying alongside Chunk 5's §15.2 grammar codemod even though this asset is otherwise designated frozen; the outcome booleans/counts are not affected. No production refs anywhere in the fixture. | RE-PINNED at WI-8.5c (Chunk 8, surface-owner rule — stays frozen-migration-input with a fresh sha256, reviewed diff): the consolidate op refs + derived lesson/knowledge refs flip to the D-R5 conceptId grammar (the parseStoredRef retirement makes op parsing new-grammar-only). The diff is PROVABLY spelling-only — every op ref, xref, and derived ref flips `<type>:<name>` -> `<stash-subdir>/<name>`; all outcome booleans/counts and untouched frontmatter are byte-identical (longer conceptIds re-wrap some inline JSON arrays, no content change).

WI-06, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R5. Preservation oracle for consolidate's run-entry recovery decision. RE-CAPTURED at WI-6.3e (Chunk 6, its owning chunk — reviewed diff + ledger entry; designation finalized at WI-6.5) on the unified FileChange transaction engine: an incomplete journal + recoveryMode 'abort' (default) still refuses the run with ConfigError INVALID_CONFIG_FILE and the same guidance, removing nothing; an unreadable journal in the SHARED per-root namespace cannot be attributed to a kind, so it warns-and-skips under 'clean' and aborts with kind-agnostic guidance otherwise (adversarial-review fix, ledgered at 6.3e); recoveryMode 'clean' removes the stale transaction dir whole (journal + backups together — the legacy scheme's two backup-timestamp derivations died with the timestamped backup dirs); and a 'completed >= operations' leftover is swept whole at the run-entry check — the legacy CHARACTERIZATION WARNING (a completed journal's backup dir leaking as a permanent orphan) is FIXED by the per-transaction-dir scheme, and this fixture pins the fix. No ref literals appear in this fixture (booleans, counts, and the ConfigError code string only) — no ref-grammar re-baseline caveat applies.

WI-06, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R5. Preservation oracle for consolidateGuardStatus's four-way verdict (consolidate/eligibility.ts:60): hot (captureMode:hot frontmatter), safe (ordinary frontmatter), unparseable (zero-key/no-frontmatter body), and missing (file absent). Prompt-level hot-list annotation is a different concern already covered by tests/commands/consolidate/consolidate-chunks.test.ts — referenced, not duplicated here. No ref literals in this fixture (verdict strings only). | RE-CAPTURED at WI-6.3e (Chunk 6, its owning chunk — reviewed diff + ledger entry; designation finalized at WI-6.5): the predicted near-no-op — the guard verdict logic is engine-independent, so only capturedAtHead moved. Designation returned to frozen-migration-input with a fresh sha256.

WI-06, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R5, §6 preserve list. The signal-delta gate SURVIVES Chunk 7's deletion of the P0-A high-retrieval lane and self-consistency voting (both pinned separately as re-baseline @ 7 by improve/self-consistency.json and improve/p0a-selection.json). Pins isSignalDeltaEligible's truth table (eligibility.ts:421-431, pure function), and the eligibleRefs/distillOnlyRefs/noFeedbackPool partition COUNTS from one full akmImprove preparation run with proactiveMaintenance disabled and zero retrievals/salience seeded so only the signal-delta gate can ever select a ref — P0-A lane membership and SC voting internals are deliberately out of scope (brief Risk 9); only the shared 'signal-delta' eligibilitySource tag (the gate's own vocabulary, not a P0-A-specific algorithm) is asserted. buildLatestFeedbackTsMap's signal/note filter and buildLatestProposalTsMap's reflect/distill cursor rules are exercised directly by the suite's assertion tests (state.db-backed) but are not separately serialized into this fixture beyond the truth-table/partition-count sections. Contains only fixture-local ref names (tests/fixtures/goldens/improve/fixture-refs.ts), booleans, and counts — no production refs.

WI-0b.4a, chunk-0b brief / docs/design/execution/chunk-0b/anchors.md Section B.1 (the 14-format producer table's Renderer column). Chunk-0b BEHAVIOR-PARITY oracle (same D0b-1/D0b-3 designation-reuse rationale as this registry's WI-0b.3 entries -- a FROZEN preservation oracle, not a migration input in the Chunk 6/7/9 sense) for `AssetRenderer.buildShowResponse(renderCtx)`'s output across all 14 ASSET_SPECS_INTERNAL types plus the workflow-program-yaml renderer form, captured against the WI-0b.2 `tests/fixtures/stashes/all-types/` fixture stash via the same buildFileContext -> runMatchers -> getRenderer -> buildRenderContext call shape src/commands/read/show.ts's showLocal uses (match.meta.name injected per-file using the SAME canonical names this registry's placement/all-types.json entry pins). The only non-deterministic field any of the 14 renderers emit is `path` (= ctx.absPath, an absolute filesystem path) -- normalized via expectGolden's roots:{stash:STASH_ROOT} substitution to the placeholder "<STASH>" (tests/_helpers/golden.ts); every other field is derived purely from the fixture's committed bytes plus the injected canonical name. Pins two documented cross-renderer asymmetries byte-for-byte (not fixed, per capture-only scope): skill/command/agent/lesson/fact/session strip frontmatter from their content/template/prompt while memory-md and knowledge-md/wiki-md's default "full" view return raw content INCLUDING the frontmatter fence; and workflow-md derives a step's title from its parsed heading while workflow-program-yaml falls back to the step id when no `title` key is set. Chunk 2's format adapters must reproduce every renderer's output byte-for-byte. anchors.md Section B.3 confirms zero prior renderer golden coverage existed before this capture -- greenfield, not a re-baseline. | RE-BASELINED at WI-8.5b (Chunk 8, surface-owner rule — reBaselineChunk moved 4 -> 8, reviewed diff): the two workflow renderers' `action` field embeds the `akm workflow next <ref>` display ref, which flips from the legacy `[origin//]workflow:name` spelling to the D-R5 display grammar (`displayRef`) with the WI-8.5b display flip. On this fixture `origin` is undefined for every case, so the two changed keys (workflows/all-types-workflow-program, workflows/all-types-workflow) render the SHORT conceptId `workflows/<name>`; every other field across all 15 keys is byte-identical.

Per the WI-0b.4 brief's explicit instruction, workflow is captured as TWO SEPARATE entries rather than one: `workflowMd` (a real lint path via WorkflowLinter) and `workflowProgramYaml` (NOT a lint path -- WorkflowLinter never sees .yaml files in production, so its only correctness surface is parseWorkflowProgram's own {ok, program|errors} result, invoked with {path: relPath} mirroring src/workflows/renderer.ts's loadProgram). LintIssue.file is always ctx.relPath (never absPath) in every linter, so no absolute path appears anywhere in this golden -- normalization is a no-op. Every fixture is lint-clean by construction, so all issues/flagged/fixed arrays are empty; this golden still pins the dispatch-table shape and full-sweep reachability, not just "zero issues". Chunk 2's format adapters must reproduce this lint dispatch byte-for-byte. anchors.md Section B.3 confirms zero prior lint golden coverage existed before this capture -- greenfield, not a re-baseline.

WI-0b.5a, chunk-0b brief / docs/design/execution/chunk-0b/anchors.md Section D.1 (the two parallel filter chains in src/indexer/search/db-search.ts: `searchDatabase` (scored, fn@:282) and `enumerateEntries` (enumerate, fn@:574)). Captured against a dedicated fixture `tests/fixtures/stashes/search-filter/` (11 memories + 1 knowledge entry; see its MANIFEST.json) purpose-built to exercise proposed/belief/scope filter combinations plus a derived-memory-twin pair. Pins 8 filter combinations (proposed-quality opt-in, belief=all/current/historical, and scope narrowing across all four StashEntryScope keys, singly and combined) run through BOTH paths via the public `akmSearch` entry point only. The verified two-path divergence is candidate-POOL membership, not filter code: searchDatabase's candidate pool comes from searchFts/vector matches for the query's own tokens (FTS indexes description/tags/searchHints/aliases, not raw body prose); enumerateEntries's candidate pool is getAllEntries for the type, independent of query text. The fixture's `silent-twin-base`/`silent-twin-base.derived` pair pins this: the twin shares NO indexed token with 'gridlock', so it is NEVER a scored-path candidate under ANY belief filter (total absence) while it is ALWAYS an enumerate-path candidate and reliably inherits its base's 'contradicted' belief state. Each hit is projected to {ref, type, name, score, quality?, beliefState?}. | RE-BASELINED @ Chunk 5 (F4b output-spelling flip; orchestrator Ruling A): the `ref` projection now emits the 0.9.0 conceptId grammar (`memory:X` → `memories/X`, `knowledge:X` → `knowledge/X`) via displayRef from db-search's resolveSearchHitRef. The re-capture diff is PURE ref-spelling — every hit's membership, order, score, quality, and beliefState are byte-identical to the frozen capture (verified by a conceptId→legacy normalized diff against the pre-flip golden: empty). Result sets and the documented scored/enumerate divergence are unchanged; only the ref string spelling moved. capturedAtHead in the fixture is retained from the frozen WI-0b.5a capture (cd25a769) — the flip touched only ref spelling, not the fixture data or search behavior. | Re-captured @chunk-8 CI-green gate (2026-07-20): enumerateEntries gained an explicit type->name->filePath listing sort. The un-ORDER-BY'd SELECT behind it returned rows in query-plan/index-insertion order, which tracks filesystem readdir order and so diverged across machines -- the previously captured enumerate halves pinned one container's order and could never pass on CI. Scored halves unchanged (that path already had the Issue #14 display-score+name tiebreak sort). Only the enumerate 'hits' arrays differ in this re-capture.
